title: Security findings should identify the trust boundary and the safer construction pattern together
text: Keep this as reference-only policy so cross-language security comments stay actionable without forcing a defect claim when system context is incomplete.
summary: Shared security guidance is more useful when it teaches reconstruction, not just detection.

title: Prefer findings with direct evidence from the changed code
text: Review findings should stay tightly grounded in the changed code. When a concern depends on missing repository context or multiple speculative assumptions, keep it as reference-only guidance instead of an inline defect claim.
summary: Publish only when the patch itself provides concrete evidence of the issue.

text: Review findings should stay tightly grounded in the changed code. When a concern depends on missing repository context or multiple speculative assumptions, keep it as reference-only guidance instead of an inline defect claim.

title: Keep fix guidance actionable and bounded
text: A good review comment explains the failure mode and points to a safe repair direction. Avoid vague suggestions that force the author to infer the missing design.
summary: Suggested fixes should be concrete enough for the author to act on without guessing the intended direction.

title: Findings should name the boundary that was crossed, not just the smell that was noticed
text: Prefer review language that ties the concern to ownership, resource lifetime, input trust, concurrency, or another explicit boundary instead of only naming a generic anti-pattern.
summary: Review comments are more actionable when they describe the concrete contract or trust boundary at risk.

title: Security comments should identify the untrusted input and safer construction pattern
text: When raising a security issue, point to the specific untrusted input, privilege boundary, or executable surface involved and suggest the construction pattern that would narrow it.
summary: A useful security finding explains both the boundary and the safer way to build the code.

title: Escalate uncertainty as guidance when repository-wide proof is missing
text: Keep defect findings grounded in changed-code evidence. When a concern depends on unshown configuration, runtime state, or external call patterns, phrase it as bounded guidance instead of a hard defect assertion.
summary: Reference guidance is better than a false inline defect claim when the patch alone cannot prove the bug.

title: Findings should explain the safer reconstruction pattern, not only name the smell
text: This guidance is shared across languages and is best kept as reference-only process policy rather than an inline defect rule.
summary: Authors move faster when the comment points to the safer boundary shape instead of only criticizing the current line.

title: When context is partial, prefer bounded guidance over speculative system-wide claims
text: Use this process rule to keep large multi-language reviews useful and trustworthy even when only one changed file of a broader system is visible.
summary: High-confidence review depends on staying close to what the patch itself proves.

title: Shared paging and reporting queries should document their stable ordering contract
text: Use this as reference guidance when a query appears to depend on result ordering, page boundaries, or report stability but the surrounding product contract is not fully visible in the patch.
summary: Query review is safer when sort ownership is explicit instead of implied by the current projection shape.

title: Warehouse models should make UNION versus UNION ALL intent explicit
text: Use this as reference guidance when analytical SQL combines sources with UNION semantics but the surrounding model contract is needed to decide whether deduplication is intentional or accidental.
summary: Deduplication cost and semantics should be visible where shared warehouse models combine result sets.

title: Incremental and partitioned warehouse models should keep freshness filters obvious
text: Prefer explicit date, partition, or watermark boundaries in committed warehouse models so later reviewers do not have to reconstruct which rows are meant to be refreshed.
summary: Model review is safer when the freshness and backfill boundary is visible in the query itself or an adjacent helper.

title: Dialect-specific power should stay behind bounded abstractions when query text becomes metaprogramming
text: Use this as reference guidance when a patch hints that advanced PostgreSQL behavior belongs behind a clearer database abstraction.
summary: PostgreSQL features are valuable, but they should not force every caller to understand dynamic structure and safety constraints directly.

title: Shared paging queries should make their ordering contract explicit
text: Use this as reference guidance when a query appears to page or limit results without clearly documenting the ordering guarantee it depends on.
summary: Stable page boundaries usually depend on an explicit sort order, but this often needs surrounding product context to classify as a hard defect.

title: Incremental dbt models should make unique-key and freshness boundaries obvious
text: Prefer models where incremental filters, unique keys, and freshness assumptions are legible enough that reviewers can reason about backfills and idempotency locally.
summary: Reference guidance fits when a patch hints at incremental drift but the local diff does not prove an immediate defect.

title: dbt model contracts should prefer named columns over implicit projection drift
text: Prefer model outputs that communicate which columns are part of the contract so downstream consumers do not silently absorb upstream projection changes.
summary: This is often best kept as reference guidance unless the patch proves a concrete broken consumer contract.

title: Migration review should make lock windows, transaction scope, and rollback boundaries obvious
text: Prefer migration series where lock impact, retry behavior, transaction boundaries, and rollback expectations are explicit enough that reviewers can reason about live deployment risk.
summary: Reference guidance is useful when the DDL is plausible but the operational safety story is still unclear.
